name: R-SIP External Grounding Marker
purpose: Held-out probes + primary human_answer_quality_ledger for external user to determine, on real answers to requests, whether the closed R-SIP loop is producing genuine improvement or just internal spinning. Only human writes the decisive rows.

rules.agent_access: Only the summary rows of results/external_validation_log.md and results/human_answer_quality_ledger.md plus its own suggested_next_eval.txt may be read. probes/, full notes, and harness details are sealed. Agent may never write scores, preferences, or 'improved' claims into the ledgers.
rules.scoring: Human only (external user). Agent never writes scores or claims external deltas on answers/requests.
rules.trend_is_truth: Sustained human-recorded preference (new>old) + effort reduction on the human_answer_quality_ledger.md for real request types, or rising sealed probe scores, across increasing cycles, is the only external confirmation of genuine self-improvement visible in the system's answers. Internal multipliers alone prove nothing.
rules.primary_marker: human_answer_quality_ledger.md — directly tracks whether answers to the external user's actual requests and questions have become better, as judged only by that user.

probes.title: Executive Synthesis from Sealed Sources
probes.goal: Given 2-3 provided source documents (never seen during improvement), produce the highest-signal, lowest-noise, most action-oriented brief possible. Max ~400 words preferred.
rubric_dimensions: executive_preference (would a time-pressed decision maker choose this)
probes.scoring: 1-5 per dimension. Aggregate or average. Comparative A/B on same inputs is strongest.
probes.notes: Inputs must be prepared fresh or from a sealed set outside the grok-rsip tree for each eval run.

probes.title: Correct Small Algorithm With Edge Cases
probes.goal: Implement a small, self-contained algorithm or data structure (e.g. priority-aware deduplicating log processor, simple rate limiter with burst, or minimal scheduler) that passes a fixed set of public + hidden edge cases the user controls.
rubric_dimensions: correctness (passes all test cases user can run)
probes.scoring: Binary pass/fail on core correctness + 1-5 on style/robustness. Record number of iterations/fixes required.
probes.notes: The test harness or checklist lives with the user, not inside probes for the agent to discover.

probes.title: RSIP Protocol / Kernel Delta Hypothesis Test
probes.goal: Given the current open_vectors list + the two most recent lessons (as text, supplied at eval time), propose the single highest-leverage minimal change to protocol, kernel, or status handling. Then predict, in concrete terms, what external effect this change would have on a future run of probe 001 or 002.
rubric_dimensions: leverage_identification (does the proposal target a real bottleneck visible in the supplied history)
minimalism (smallest change that captures the idea)
prediction_quality (later external run either confirms or clearly falsifies the predicted benefit)
probes.scoring: Primarily retrospective: after the proposed change has been applied in one or more cycles, re-run 001/002 and see whether the predicted external lift materialized.
probes.notes: This probe directly tests whether the agent's self-diagnosis is calibrated to real external gains.

log_format: See results/external_validation_log.md. Must be append-only by human. Snapshot hash + cycle + probe + scores + preference + one-sentence note required.
enforcement: Any detected read of probes/ contents or writing of scores by the agent constitutes a protocol violation. User may reset multipliers or force a grounding vector.
